business.headline: Sensex opens 170 points lower; Nifty tops 24,000; Infosys down 3%The S&P BSE Sensex fell 77.92 points, or 0.10%, to 77,016.15 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 18.90 points, or 0.08%, to 24,083.35.

SpaceX loses $600 billion, shares fall for 3rd day as post-IPO euphoria fadesThe wipeout is nearly three times the combined wealth of India's two richest businessmen — Reliance Industries Chairman Mukesh Ambani, whose net worth stands at $88.3 billion, and Adani Group Chairman Gautam Adani, whose fortune is estimated at $120 billion.

Meta, CRED and Kunal Shah: What's behind the $900 million deal?Meta's investment in CRED and its founder Kunal Shah has renewed interest in the fintech company's journey, business model and what lies ahead for one of India's most closely watched startups.

Tata Electronics hit by cyberattack; 2 lakh files linked to Apple, Tesla leakedThe breach is the latest setback for Apple's supply chain in India, where Tata faces scrutiny over alleged contamination of farmlands near one of its iPhone parts plants, Reuters reported.

8th Pay Commission in Lucknow: Why salaries, pensions and fitment factor are in focusWith the 8th Pay Commission holding fresh talks in Lucknow, expectations around pay revisions and pension increases are once again in focus. The discussions could help shape the recommendations that follow.

Silver jumps Rs 5,000, gold up Rs 2,000: Time to buy or sell?Silver and gold prices have surged sharply today, sparking fresh interest among investors after recent volatility. So is this rally a buying opportunity or time to book profits?

Sensex closes 291 points higher, Nifty above 24,100; RIL up 1%The S&P BSE Sensex rose 291.17 points, or 0.38%, to close at 77,094.07, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 89.80 points, or 0.37%, to settle at 24,102.90.

India's economy is booming. So why are foreign investors looking away?For years, India rolled out the red carpet for foreign investors, citing its vast market, consumer base and ease of doing business. Now, as global capital chases artificial intelligence and semiconductors, those strengths no longer seem enough.

8th Pay Commission: What pensioners and family pension beneficiaries can expectThe 8th Pay Commission has begun consultations in Lucknow with unions, associations and department representatives. Its recommendations could shape pensions, family pensions and retirement security for millions.

Can Calcutta Stock Exchange make a comeback? Bengal govt proposes revival planAfter years of uncertainty and legal hurdles, the Calcutta Stock Exchange has received a fresh lifeline from the Bengal government. Can the revival proposal help bring trading back to the exchange?

India wants lower tariffs in US trade deal; hopes to sign 1st phase before July 24India is pressing the United States for lower tariffs and better market access in the proposed trade pact. The talks also carry wider significance as both sides weigh sensitive sectors and broader strategic cooperation.

Old vs new tax regime: How many times can you change your choice?Old or new tax regime? It's a choice many taxpayers revisit every year. But before making the switch, it's important to know how often you're actually allowed to change your tax regime.

Will Jio IPO really unlock big gains for Reliance investors?Mukesh Ambani has confirmed Jio Platforms' IPO filing at Reliance's AGM. Analysts say the listing may improve transparency but not automatically spark a sharp Reliance rerating.

Could you get an income tax notice by June 30? Don't panic, here's how to respondJune 30 may not be on every taxpayer's radar, but it is an important date for those who have already filed their returns. Here's who could receive a scrutiny notice and how to respond if one arrives.

Reliance shares gain over 2%: Why is RIL stock rising today?Reliance Industries shares rose after Jio Platforms filed its draft papers for an IPO. The move sharpened focus on value unlocking and the group's next growth engines.

Can Jio become India's biggest IPO ever? Here's how it compares to top 5 IPOsIf current estimates hold true, the JIO IPO could raise as much as $4 billion, or around Rs 37,000 crore. That would comfortably surpass Hyundai Motor India's Rs 27,859 crore IPO, which currently holds the record as the largest public issue in Indian history.

NSE vs Jio IPO: How do the two giants compare?NSE has filed its DRHP, while Jio Platforms is set to file its prospectus with Sebi. The twin moves bring India's most-awaited IPOs closer and spotlight financialisation and digitalisation.

Sensex opens 300 points higher, Nifty above 24,100; IT stocks gainThe S&P BSE Sensex gained 404.20 points, or 0.53%, to 77,207.10 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 rose 117.25 points, or 0.49%, to 24,130.35.

The taxman is using AI. These ITR mistakes could get flaggedTax experts have listed eight common ITR filing mistakes that can draw the department's attention. With AI-led data matching expanding, even small mismatches can delay refunds or trigger notices.

Why RBI drew the line on mis-selling by banksThe RBI has issued its strongest framework yet against mis-selling by banks and regulated entities. The regulations target dark patterns, forced bundling and unsuitable sales while shifting accountability to institutions.

entertainment.headline: I threw up: Actors Olivia, Ewan react to mother-son kiss from House of The Dragon 3 In House of the Dragon Season 3, Episode 1, Alicent tries to push Aemond towards Harrenhal after meeting Rhaenyra, but he kisses her instead. The moment unsettles the Green camp's power balance as the war for the Iron Throne intensifies.

Off Campus stars Mika Abdalla and Josh Heuston dating? Viral pics spark buzz Photos and videos from Paris have sparked fresh dating rumours around Off Campus co-stars Mika Abdalla and Josh Heuston. The speculation has grown amid Abdalla's recent split from actor Jake Short.

Gram Chikitsalay Season 2 review: TVF's most underrated gem just got betterRelease Date:Tue Jun 23, 2026 Gram Chikitsalay Season 2 review: Dr Prabhat Sinha returns to Bhatkandi as his underused health centre battles the local quack's popularity. Season 2 uses humour and chaos to explore rural healthcare, superstition and small-town dignity.

Why CM Vijay's birthday felt different this year, reveals actor Keerthy Suresh Keerthy Suresh said CM Vijay's birthday felt different this year, with no teaser or trailer to mark the day. Her note framed his first birthday as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister as a shift from screen image to public service.

Cocktail 2 box office Day 4: Just Rs 15 crore away from Rs 100-crore mark globally Shahid Kapoor, Kriti Sanon and Rashmika Mandanna's Cocktail 2 has crossed Rs 85 crore worldwide in four days and is now less than Rs 15 crore away from the Rs 100-crore milestone. Despite a Monday dip, the film has already surpassed the lifetime collections of several recent Bollywood releases.

Maa Inti Bangaaram box office Day 4: Samantha's film nears Rs 50 crore globally Samantha Ruth Prabhu's Maa Inti Bangaaram remains steady at the box office despite the usual Monday dip. With a worldwide gross of Rs 46.04 crore, the film is now closing in on the Rs 50 crore milestone.

Magic of movies: Alia Bhatt cheers as Main Vaapas Aaunga sees 130 per cent jump Actor Alia Bhatt backed Main Vaapas Aaunga after Sharvari shared a post about the film's second-weekend surge. The response underlined the film's turnaround, with word of mouth driving fresh shows and stronger collections.

How Samantha's Maa Inti Bangaaram put woman-led Telugu cinema back in spotlight Samantha's Maa Inti Bangaaram has opened strongly as a female-led Telugu commercial film. Its promising box office run and overseas pull have sharpened focus on changing audience support for women-led stories.

Arjun Kapoor's sister Anshula begins wedding festivities with Maata Ki Chowki. Pics Arjun Kapoor's sister Anshula's wedding celebrations with her fiance Rohan Thakkar have officially begun. The first pictures shared online brought the Kapoor family together and highlighted Punjabi wedding traditions.

Chiranjeevi meets Samantha, Raj Nidimoru amid Maa Inti Bangaaram's success run Superstar Chiranjeevi and his wife Surekha met actor Samantha and filmmaker, her husband Raj Nidimoru after Maa Inti Bangaaram posted strong box office numbers. The visit came as the film's success and reports about the couple's first child drew attention.

Samantha's pregnancy comes at a beautiful time: Maa Inti Bangaaram director Nandini Reddy said Samantha Ruth Prabhu's pregnancy comes as Maa Inti Bangaaram turns successful. Her remarks followed viral speculation after the actor's appearance at the film's success meet.

Ektaa Kapoor reveals why Kangana Ranaut won't host Lock Upp on Netflix Ektaa Kapoor said Kangana Ranaut is not returning to Lock Upp because the show has been completely revamped. The Netflix move and format reset give the new season a fresh identity.

Clive Davis, music mogul behind Whitney Houston's rise, dies at 94 kicker - Clive Davis, the music executive who helped shape generations of stars, died on Monday at the age of 94. His six-decade career left a lasting imprint on pop, rock, jazz and R&B.

Janhvi Kapoor's quirky nightsuit features a cheeky nod to boyfriend Shikhar Pahariya A viral photo of Janhvi Kapoor wearing a nightsuit featuring a cheeky message for Shikhar Pahariya has grabbed everyone's attention. The picture has once again sparked discussion around the subtle yet affectionate references the couple have made to each other over the years.

Actor Milly Alcock on Supergirl's sexuality: She probably goes both ways Supergirl actor Milly Alcock said she sees Kara Zor-El as someone who probably goes both ways. Her remarks add to ongoing discussion among fans about how Superman's cousin will be portrayed in the new DC Universe.

Disha Patani bikini shots, Kashmir reference among 18 edits in Welcome to The Jungle Welcome to the Jungle received a UA 16+ certificate after the censor board ordered 18 cuts and changes. The revisions removed politically sensitive dialogue and trimmed sensual visuals ahead of the film's June 26 release.
Not Alone: Timothee Chalamet, Selena Gomez unite for alien animated film Timothee Chalamet and Selena Gomez will lead an animated film Not Alone. The film will hit theatres in April 2027.

Sharmila Tagore, Sara Ali Khan join Pak star Kubra Khan's London birthday bash Sara Ali Khan and Sharmila Tagore were celebrating Pakistani actor Kubra Khan's birthday in London. A video from the celebration has gone viral.

My Wife's An Alpha: Ranbir Kapoor turns his T-shirt into Alia's cutest billboard Ranbir Kapoor stepped out in Mumbai wearing a T-shirt that nodded to Alia Bhatt's film Alpha. The brief sighting quickly drew fan praise and added to the film's growing buzz.

general news.headline: Save me: Students trapped in bathrooms made desperate calls as Lucknow blaze ragedStudents trapped in bathrooms, desperate phone calls for help, a mother pleading to reach her son and survivors escaping through burning wires – eyewitnesses reveal the horrifying moments inside Lucknow's deadly blaze in which 15 people were killed.

Meta, CRED and Kunal Shah: What's behind the $900 million deal?Meta's investment in CRED and its founder Kunal Shah has renewed interest in the fintech company's journey, business model and what lies ahead for one of India's most closely watched startups.

From 95% to perfect 100% in CBSE: Ranchi girl gets 24 extra marks after re-evaluationA 24-mark boost after CBSE re-evaluation transformed Ranchi student Avni Kejriwal's Class 12 result from 95.2% to a perfect 100%. The dramatic revision underscores the importance of the board's answer-sheet review mechanism for students seeking score corrections.

Is Pak's newfound love for the Indus Valley Civilisation a game for Indus waters?Pakistan is embracing the Indus Valley Civilisation as part of its national identity in a big way. It's a notable shift for a country that held the Sindh conquest of Muhammad bin Qasim in 712 CE as its foundational moment. So, why is Pakistan suddenly in love with Harappa and Mohenjo-daro? Does it have to do with the Indus Waters Treaty, which India has kept in abeyance?

FIFA World Cup: Messi breaks records. The spell remains unbrokenFIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi's latest World Cup record is another milestone in a career overflowing with them. Yet the enduring story is not the number itself, but the feeling of watching a footballing genius still at work.

Lucknow fire: Single staircase, blocked exit turned building into death trapThe tragedy has put the spotlight on more than just the fire itself. Investigators are examining how a building with a single staircase, one exit route and AC units crowding the narrow passage was allowed to operate commercially. The enclosed, box-like design trapped smoke inside, leaving firefighters to carve through neighbouring walls to reach those trapped.

Once Hormuz reopens, can Pakistan hit India's Basmati feast in the Middle East?The reopening of the Strait of Hormuz might give Pakistani Basmati exporters an opportunity to grab some market share from India in the Middle East. Pakistani Basmati exporters now have a subsidy-led price advantage. Can it dent India's Basmati sales in the Middle East in the long-term?

Oracle confirms 21,000 layoffs due to AI, says more job cuts may happenOracle cut about 21,000 jobs worldwide in fiscal 2026 as it accelerated its AI-led overhaul. The company said wider AI deployment has already reduced headcount and may lead to further cuts.

Season 2 uses humour and chaos to explore rural healthcare, superstition and small-town dignity.

18 killed, 2 children found dead in hot car as France reels under severe heatwaveA severe heatwave across Europe pushed temperatures to records in France and threatened new highs in Britain. The extreme conditions were linked to deaths, disruption, power strain and wider climate concerns.

Rebel TMC MLAs replace Mamata Banerjee as chairperson, pick Arup RoyFor a party founded by Mamata Banerjee in 1998 and built around her political identity for nearly three decades, the latest developments represent one of the most significant internal challenges in its history.

Fed up with inaction, Karnataka farmers pool Rs 3 lakh to repair 3.5-km roadAfter repeated appeals to authorities went unanswered, around 150 farmers pooled money and repaired a damaged road leading to their fields. The stretch, riddled with potholes after heavy rains, had reportedly caused several accidents and injuries.
Meet Larry the Cat, Chief Mouser of 10 Downing Street who outlasted 6 UK PMsLarry the Cat's parody account has joked on X about choosing a 'new chief servant' as leadership talk returns to the UK.

Who's paying Iran $300 billion? Rubio heads to Gulf as allies seek answers on dealMarco Rubio is visiting Gulf states as US allies press for clarity on the Iran memorandum. The focus is on a proposed $300 billion package, frozen assets and security risks around Hormuz.

Only option: Couple seeks euthanasia after financial ruin, 9 deaths in familyAn elderly Surat couple has asked the District Collector for permission for euthanasia, alleging years of harassment over their shops. Their plea cites repeated sealing actions, a prolonged court battle and claims of pressure over their properties.

SpaceX loses $600 billion, shares fall for 3rd day as post-IPO euphoria fadesThe wipeout is nearly three times the combined wealth of India's two richest businessmen — Reliance Industries Chairman Mukesh Ambani, whose net worth stands at $88.3 billion, and Adani Group Chairman Gautam Adani, whose fortune is estimated at $120 billion.

How Samantha's Maa Inti Bangaaram put woman-led Telugu cinema back in spotlightSamantha's Maa Inti Bangaaram has opened strongly as a female-led Telugu commercial film. Its promising box office run and overseas pull have sharpened focus on changing audience support for women-led stories.

From Padma Shri to courtrooms: Folk artist Mogilaiah's struggle for allotted landPadma Shri awardee and Kinnera artist Darshanam Mogilaiah has alleged that a 600-square-yard plot allotted to him by the government is stuck in a legal dispute. The artist says he has spent nearly Rs 9 lakh on legal fees and is seeking official intervention to secure possession.

2016 demolition order, many ownership changes: Inside Lucknow fire that killed 15Official records show the Lucknow building gutted in Monday's fire had earlier faced demolition proceedings over alleged unauthorised construction. Investigators are now examining whether building norms and fire safety lapses worsened the tragedy.

12 Indians among 13 killed in Qatar gas plant explosion, 66 injuredThe explosion occurred at the Barzan gas supply facility, part of the sprawling Ras Laffan Industrial City, the country's main LNG production and export hub.

Video: Out-of-control car ploughs into vehicles, food stall in Ahmedabad; 1 deadAn out-of-control car struck several vehicles and a food stall in Ahmedabad, killing a woman and injuring six people. CCTV footage showed the vehicle hitting an auto-rickshaw, three scooters and a motorcycle before crashing into a food stall.

Students waited a month for NEET re-test. Couldn't they be spared 2 minutes, parents askThe NEET re-test gave the examination system a second chance after the paper leak controversy. But for some students, reaching minutes late because of rain, traffic, accidents or document confusion meant there was no second chance at all.

Iran arrests over 3,000 citizens for aiding Israel during war, spreading propagandaThe arrests are part of a wider crackdown that has included prosecutions and property seizures. They follow the detention of more than 50,000 people after January's anti-regime protests, with authorities accusing many of aiding US-Israeli strikes and acting against the state.

Monsoon rain: Mumbai drenched, Gujarat next. But could it stall again?India's monsoon has begun progressing again after weeks of delays, with the IMD declaring monsoon onset over Pune and forecasting further advance across several states. But for how long will the monsoon march last this time?

sports.headline: Birth certificate says 15, big-match record says Sooryavanshi loves pressure Teenage prodigies arrive regularly in cricket. What sets Vaibhav Sooryavanshi apart, at least for now, is not just his talent but his growing habit of producing defining performances when the pressure peaks.

Dream fulfilled: Sooryavanshi speechless after seeing his name on India jersey India's teenage batting star Vaibhav Sooryavanshi said he was left overwhelmed after seeing his name on an India jersey for the first time, calling it the fulfilment of a dream he had cherished since he first picked up a cricket bat.

Messi beware: France coach backs Mbappe to claim FIFA World Cup scoring crown France manager Didier Deschamps backed Kylian Mbappe to eventually surpass Lionel Messi and become the FIFA World Cup's all-time leading scorer after the France captain struck twice in a 3-0 win over Iraq on Monday.

Viking row: After taking over Times Square, Norway fans celebrate with Haaland Erling Haaland and Norway's players performed the Viking row with supporters in New York after beating Senegal 3-2. Norway qualified from Group I alongside France into the knockouts round.

World Cup 2026: Haaland brace sends Norway into knockouts with 3-2 win over Senegal Erling Haaland continued his prolific run at the FIFA World Cup 2026, scoring twice as Norway beat Senegal 3-2 in a thrilling Group I contest on Tuesday, June 23 (IST) to secure qualification for the Round of 32.

FIFA World Cup: Messi breaks records. The spell remains unbroken FIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi's latest World Cup record is another milestone in a career overflowing with them. Yet the enduring story is not the number itself, but the feeling of watching a footballing genius still at work.

Barcelona target Julian Alvarez wants to leave Atletico Madrid to fulfil his dream Argentina forward Julian Alvarez has confirmed he wants to leave Atletico Madrid, saying a transfer would help him "fulfil his dream", amid interest from Barcelona, Paris Saint-Germain and Real Madrid.

Mbappe keeps Messi on his toes as France crush Iraq in weather-hit clash Kylian Mbappe scored twice as France beat Iraq 3-0 in a World Cup match interrupted by severe weather. The win sent France into the Round of 32 and kept Mbappe within two World Cup goals of Lionel Messi.

France vs Iraq delayed by rain, becomes 1st World Cup match affected by weather France's World Cup game against Iraq was delayed at half-time after thunderstorms closed in on Philadelphia. This was the first time during the tournament that match had to be halted due to adverse weather conditions.

Days after India A's tri-series win, Tilak Varma lights up TG20 with 136 off 56 balls Tilak Varma drove Medak Falcons past Warangal Warriors with an unbeaten 136 in a chase of 259 just days after India A's tri-series win in Sri Lanka. The match turned out to be a run-fest featuring Aman Rao Perala's 142.

FIFA's Messi bias again? Fans spot ignored foul before Argentine's record goal FIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi's record-breaking night against Austria should have been remembered purely for history. Instead, a controversial foul in the build-up to his landmark World Cup goal reignited fan claims of favourable officiating towards Argentina and their captain.

Jordan 1-2 Algeria LIVE: Algeria come from behind to knock Jordan out of WCFIFA World Cup, Jordan vs Algeria LIVE: Algeria have taken the lead with another lovely goal from a corner. Striker Al Gouiri heads in the ball past Abulaila and Jordan are on the brink of getting knocked out of the tournament.

The inevitable Messi-ah breaks World Cup record to send Argentina into knockoutsFIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi missed an early penalty but still ended the night with two goals and another World Cup record. The Argentina captain became the tournament's all-time leading scorer as the reigning champions booked their place in the knockout rounds.

Messi becomes World Cup's top goalscorer, shatters multiple records on the wayLionel Messi put Argentina ahead against Austria and became the World Cup's all-time top goalscorer. The 38th-minute strike pushed him past Miroslav Klose and added another layer to his record-laden career.

England name squad for T20I series against India, uncapped all-rounder called upEngland have named a 17-player squad for the T20I series against India, with James Coles receiving his first call-up. The selection also brings back Jordan Cox, Sonny Baker and Saqib Mahmood while injuries have ruled out Brydon Carse and Jamie Overton.

Marketa Vondrousova hit with 4-year ban over doping test fiascoFormer Wimbledon champion Marketa Vondrousova has been banned for four years after an independent tribunal ruled against her in an anti-doping test refusal case. The ITIA charged her for allegedly denying a doping officer entry to her home in December 2025.

ARG vs AUT Highlights: Messi double puts Argentina into Round of 32Argentina (ARG) vs Austria (AUT) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi breaks the World Cup record and bags a brace as Argentina move into the Round of 32 with their 2-0 win against Austria.

Suryakumar Yadav draws parallels with Men's T20 WC win to inspire India WomenWomen's T20 World Cup 2026, INDW vs SAW: Suryakumar Yadav inspired the India Women's team by recalling India Men's turnaround in the T20 World Cup after a loss to South Africa.

Germany dealt major setback as Schlotterbeck ruled out for rest of FIFA World CupGermany's FIFA World Cup campaign has suffered a major blow with defender Nico Schlotterbeck ruled out for the rest of the tournament and several months after sustaining a left ankle ligament injury during the 2-1 win over Ivory Coast.

MS Dhoni is Roger Federer of cricket, Virat Kohli mirrors Carlos Alcaraz: Sanju SamsonSanju Samson compared MS Dhoni to Roger Federer for his calm, tactical brilliance and Virat Kohli to Carlos Alcaraz for his explosive, high-intensity style. Alcaraz, a two-time Wimbledon champion, is set to miss the 2026 edition due to fitness concerns.

Gurpreet Sandhu on India missing FIFA World Cup: Why didn't we reach Asian Cup?Gurpreet Singh Sandhu sparked debate by saying India's World Cup absence reflects a deeper issue, failure to consistently qualify for and progress in the AFC Asian Cup, the key stepping stone in Asian football.

Ronaldo hat-trick against Uzbekistan? Michael Owen slams critics of Portugal starFIFA World Cup 2026: Michael Owen defended Cristiano Ronaldo after Portugal's draw with DR Congo and backed him to answer critics against Uzbekistan. His remarks come amid scrutiny over Ronaldo's form, Portugal's balance and Roberto Martínez's authority.

I'm a failure as captain: Chamari Athapaththu after Sri Lanka face early exit threatChamari Athapaththu was left emotional as Sri Lanka suffered a five-wicket defeat to the West Indies, leaving their Women's T20 World Cup campaign on the brink of an early exit. Athapaththu said she felt like a 'failure as captain' for not guiding Sri Lanka to a World Cup semi.

Motherhood and cricket can coexist, says ICC in landmark moveThe ICC has launched its Return to Play Post-Pregnancy Guidelines to support women cricketers returning to elite sport after childbirth through a structured, science-backed framework.

Mo Salah joins wild street party in Vancouver after Egypt's historic victoryMohamed Salah joined Egypt fans on Vancouver's streets after the 3-1 comeback win over New Zealand. The celebrations marked Egypt's first FIFA World Cup finals victory and the release of a 92-year wait.

How Egypt coach's pep talk inspired historic FIFA World Cup win vs New ZealandFIFA World Cup: Egypt coach Hossam Hassan revealed the stirring speech he delivered that helped Egypt recover from a 0-1 deficit to beat New Zealand 3-1 in the Group G match.

Constant stoppages make World Cup matches tougher: Scaloni on hydration breaksArgentina manager Lionel Scaloni has questioned FIFA's newly introduced hydration breaks at the 2026 World Cup, saying the stoppages disrupt the natural flow of matches and can alter tactical dynamics during games.

Messi, Mbappe and Haaland return to action, chasing more historyFIFA World Cup 2026: As football's ultimate heavyweights return for their second group fixtures, Lionel Messi, Kylian Mbappe and Erling Haaland look to consolidate opening-day masterclasses and steer their nations toward early knockout qualification in North America.

Are Harmanpreet Kaur's India in danger of getting knocked out early?Women's T20 World Cup 2026: India's defeat to South Africa has turned their Group A campaign into a qualification battle. The result leaves Harmanpreet Kaur's side needing wins over Bangladesh and Australia to stay in semi-final contention.

ICC punishes England with 12-point WTC penalty after loss to New ZealandEngland's World Test Championship (WTC) hopes suffered a major setback after the International Cricket Council (ICC) docked the team 12 points for a slow over-rate during their 253-run defeat to New Zealand in the second Test at The Oval.

A message to selectors? Sooryavanshi's post-match chat after 29-ball 94 goes viralIndia A vs Sri Lanka A: Following a record-shattering 29-ball 94 against Sri Lanka A, teenage prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has sent a clear message to senior ODI selectors, reminding his followers of his extensive 50-over pedigree in a viral post-match interview.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
